opt.reducemotion.title: Stops looping animations like gauge blinking and badge pulsing

sel.name: Item
lang.confirm: Changing the language will reload the game. Continue?
auto.unlocked.title: 🤖 Auto-Play Unlocked
auto.unlocked.body:
Ten days survived. The routine lives in your hands now.

With auto-play on, every hour handles treating → cleaning → the best expedition for you. Toggle it anytime with the 🤖 button or in settings.
auto.unlocked.toast: 🤖 Auto-play on — the shelter runs itself now
display.title: 🖥️ Display
widget.title: 🪟 Widget Mode
widget.clickthrough.title: While on, you cannot interact with the game. Turn it off from the tray/taskbar.
widget.clickthrough.confirm: Turning on click-through lets mouse clicks pass through the game. It will auto-disable after 10 seconds for safety. Continue?
intro.0:
Three years since the world fell, at the tail end of winter.
Half-melted snow still clings to the cracked roads,
where early spring grass has just begun to push through.
intro.1:
You survived.
And then you realized —
surviving alone is not enough.
intro.2:
In the middle of the wasteland, one abandoned container.
From today, this is your home.

A home of my own, amid the ruins.
ending.line0:
As the roar of the helicopter faded,
the scientist gazed silently at your home for a long while.
ending.line1:
"{day} days... you truly lived out
all those years here?"
ending.line2:
You looked back at your home one last time.
The well-worn furniture, the light by the window, every scratch on the wall.
ending.line2cat:
The cat leapt into your arms as if it were the most natural thing.
It comes with you.
ending.line3:
The ruins grew smaller and smaller.

But that home — it was, without a doubt, yours.
ending.line4:
🚁 ENDING — Beyond the Ruins

Survived {day} days · {succ} successful expeditions{cat}
ending.note: 🚁 You were rescued, yet in the end you returned home.
ending.epilogue: 🏠 Epilogue — you chose this home among the ruins
toast.exhausted: You are exhausted — eat and drink before you can move
toast.expLimit: You have already been out {n} times today — 🛌 you must rest
exp.fatigue: Dead tired — better turn in early tonight
sleep.cantDuringExp: You can't sleep until you return from the expedition
sleep.noteFloor: 😴 Curled up and slept on the floor. (energy {e})
sleep.autoBed: 😴 Collapsed from exhaustion... woke in the bed to morning (⚡{e})
sleep.autoFloor: 😴 Collapsed from exhaustion... woke on the floor to morning (⚡{e})
sleep.wakeBed: 😴 Slept soundly in the bed — morning has come (⚡{e})
sleep.wakeFloor: 😴 Curled up on the floor — morning has come (⚡{e})
day.napMorning: 😴 Sleep finally won — you wake and it's morning (⚡{e})
day.expNight: 🌒 You scavenged through the night — back only at first light
sleep.noteCollapse: 😵 Finally collapsed into sleep — waking, it's already morning (⚡{e})
sleep.collapse: 😵 Held out till dawn, then collapsed into sleep (⚡{e})
sleep.confirm: Sleeping now restores energy {cur} → {to}. Turn in?
eat.noFood: No food — explore the residential district
eat.doneCanned: 🥫 Had a meal of canned food (+45)
opt.autoplay.title: Available from Day 10 — automatically handles treatment, cleaning, and expeditions
btn.auto.title: Auto-play (Day 10+)
btn.edit.title: Arrange mode — move/reclaim furniture
edit.on: 🔧 Arrange mode — you can move and reclaim furniture
btn.uiToggle.title: Hide / show game UI
drop.child: {emoji} {name} lost its spot and was reclaimed
save.corrupt: ⚠️ Save was corrupted — restored from yesterday’s backup
err.global: ⚠️ An error occurred, but your save is safe
btn.saveNow.title: Save now — this save can be loaded from the lobby
slot.del.title: Delete save
slot.delConfirm: Delete the save in slot {n}? This cannot be undone.
slot.newConfirm: Overwrite the existing save in slot {n} and start anew?
slot.hardBadge.title: Hard mode
mode.pick.title: Choose Your Winter
mode.normal.tag: Warmth still lingers in the ruins
mode.normal.desc: Standard balance. Cozy survival at your own pace.
mode.hard.tag: The ruins give nothing for free
mode.hard.desc: Expedition loot −30% · hunger/thirst drain +50% · cold snaps strike more often. The first winter becomes a real test.
mode.zen.tag: No end, no rush — the ruins keep your hours
mode.zen.desc: Normal balance with a generous start. Auto-play unlocked from day one. Made for idling.
slot.zenBadge.title: Endless mode
mode.hardcore.tag: The ruins do not bury you twice
mode.hardcore.desc: Hard balance, and no rescue. No parcel at the door, no sound of a helicopter. The only warmth is what you kindle yourself.
mode.wallpaper.desc: Resources endless (∞), every shelter open. No gauges, no winter pressure — only placing and decorating. Seasons and weather simply drift by; a home to leave glowing on your desktop.
mode.wallpaper.lock: It belongs to those who have lived {n} days in Normal.
mode.wallpaper.lockToast: Opens after passing day {n} in Normal.
slot.hardcoreBadge.title: Hardcore mode
slot.wallpaperBadge.title: Wallpaper mode
wallpaper.noAction: In Wallpaper mode, no one heads out to scavenge.
rescue.parcel.title: A Parcel at the Door
rescue.parcel.body:
At dawn, a worn box sat by the door. No telling who left it.
Inside, enough to last a few days. No note, no name.

Someone you never met kindled this warmth, just once, in your place.
rescue.parcel.note: 🎁 Someone left a parcel at the door.
rescue.heli.title: A Far-off Rotor
rescue.heli.body:
A rotor passed beyond the roof. Soon a single chute settles onto the snow.
The helicopter never stopped. It left one supply crate behind.

A warmth that only grazed you held you up, just once.
rescue.heli.note: 🚁 A helicopter dropped a supply crate.
ended.title: The Last Page
ended.page1:
The record reaches this far.

Where the pen stopped, a day quietly closed. The warmth this home once held stays on, as a record.
ended.toTitle: The record remains in its slot.
inv.collectAll.title: Gather all {n} placed pieces.
shelter.leftover: The {n} pieces here stay in inventory when you move.
map.title: 🗺️ Expedition Map
map.sketchInfo: Not yet visited — rumors only. Come back and it stays in ink.
prep.title: {emoji} {name} — Expedition Prep
exp.partialBody: It was risky, but you salvaged a little.
exp.failBagBody: A wash — but the bag caught a few useful things on the way back.
exp.note.bag: 🎒 Thanks to the bag, you didn’t come back empty.
exp.failSalvageBody: A failure, but with a sailor’s grit you brought a few things back.
exp.note.loot2: 📡 The broadcast’s coordinates were true — you recovered double the supplies from the cache!
exp.note.furniture: 🛋️ You found an intact piece of furniture in the ruins — a rare stroke of luck!
exp.note.book: 📕 You salvaged a book that survived the damp — knowledge left in the ruins.
exp.note.rooftopSalvage: 📡 Thanks to the rooftop’s vantage, you salvaged a piece of furniture.
exp.note.shipSalvage: 🎣 Lifeboat locker trait — recovered some supplies even in failure.
exp.note.dirty5: 🧹 You came back covered in dirt and cleanliness dropped. (-5)
exp.note.firstaid: ⛑️ The first-aid kit turned a deep wound into a minor injury.
exp.note.gloves: 🧤 The gloves kept you from getting hurt.
exp.note.wet3: {icon} You came back soaked and cleanliness dropped. (-3)
exp.unlock: 🔓 New shelter discovered: {emoji} {name} — relocate via the [🏠 Relocate] menu!
exp.cause.injured: 🩹 The injury dragged at you the whole way.
exp.cause.flashlight: 🔦 The flashlight let you search every dark corner.
exp.cause.raincoat: {icon} The raincoat took the worst of the weather for you.
exp.cause.bottle: 🥤 The water bottle kept your throat from burning dry.
exp.cause.canned: 🥫 Canned rations held the hunger back.
injury.applied: {icon} You suffered {name} — expedition success -{pen}%p, ~{h}h (game) until natural recovery.
injury.card:
{icon} {name} — expedition success -{pen}%p{time}
~{h} until natural recovery (rest)
move.journeyNote: 🚶 Traveled to {name} — the journey took 3 hours.
move.confirmFurniture: Your {n} placed furnishings will stay behind. Move anyway?
move.newShelter: 🏠 New shelter found — check the relocation map
move.badge.title: A shelter is ready to relocate to
edit.enterAsk: {name} — you need placement mode to move or reclaim it.
edit.enterHint: 🔧 You can move it in placement mode
shelter.intro: You can move freely within a district, but relocating to another district costs supplies (🥫1+💧1) and time (3h). Moving into a shelter for the first time requires refit resources. Placed furniture stays with each shelter; your inventory is shared.
craft.title: 🔨 Workbench
craft.intro: Craft supplies and furniture from materials gathered on expeditions.
craft.modIntro: Modifications are permanently installed in this shelter.
craft.noMods: No modifications available for this shelter.
btn.wardrobe.title: Wardrobe — change into crafted clothes
wardrobe.title: Wardrobe
wardrobe.hint: Clothes are sewn from cloth at the bench. Tapping yourself in the scene opens this too.
journal.title: 📖 Survival Journal
journal.statsLine: Day {day} {sicon} · {exp} expeditions ({succ} succeeded) · {craft} crafts · {stay} days settled
deco.intro: Change the walls and floor of this shelter. Remembered per shelter.
deco.noWall: Glass walls leave nowhere for wallpaper. The floor is fair game.
comfort.breakdownTitle: Comfort {score} — what makes this place livable
event.stolen:
Under cover of darkness, 1 {name} was stolen.
Best to keep a light on at night.
event.chip.title: Set-aside event — click to reopen
place.noStock: No {name} in stock — 🎒 obtain it on an expedition
place.maxItems: The {shelter} holds at most {n} pieces of furniture
settled.8: 🏡 Now it truly feels like home. (settled +8, max)
day.genRun: ⚡ Generator running — battery use is free today. (fuel -1)
day.genStop: ⚠️ Out of fuel — the generator stopped.
day.autopurifier: ⛲ The auto water station drew up water. (+{n})
day.knowWater: 💧 Gathered and filtered dew and rain. (+{n})
day.gardenFrozen: ❄️ Winter — the garden is frozen; no harvest.
day.foodSpoiled: 🍎 Without a fridge, fresh food spoiled. (-1)
day.foodSpoiledSummer: 🍎 In the summer heat, fresh food spoiled faster.
day.foodFresh: 🧊 Thanks to the fridge, food stayed fresh.
day.seaDamp: 💧 Sea damp leaves the shelter clammy.
day.openWet: {icon} The open-air shelter was soaked by the weather.
day.roofRepair: 🔧 Bad weather — repaired the roof with 1 material.
day.rooftopRain: 🌧️ The rooftop catchment collected clean water. (+{n})
day.bigraincatch: 🛢️ The large rain catch filled up with water. (+{n})
day.gardenBoxFrozen: 🌱 The garden box is frozen; no harvest.
day.solar: 🔆 The solar panel charged a battery. (+1)
day.surplusSold: 📕 Traded surplus food at the black market for {b} book(s). (food -{n})
day.veryDirty: 🧹 The shelter is very dirty. It needs cleaning.
day.upkeepUnpaid: ⚠️ Upkeep unpaid ({label}) — shelter comfort bonus is off.
day.infectWorse: 🤒 The neglected wound worsened into an infection risk.
day.deepWorse: 🩸 The wound went untended and cut deeper.
day.criticalWorse: 🚑 The wound festered. You can barely stand.
day.catHint: 🐈 A faint mew drifts in from somewhere...
day.catPrints: 🐾 Small paw prints dot the snow by the door. Something passed through in the night.
day.cat0: 🐈 The cat basked by the window all day.
day.cat1: 🐈 The cat left a mouse at the door. A gift, it seems.
day.cat2: 🐈 The cat claimed the middle of the bed. You slept in the corner.
day.cat3: 🐈 The cat found a ball of yarn and batted it around all night.
day.catJoined: 🐈 The cat has come to live with you.
day.catHungry: 🐈 The cat looks hungry... (no food — its comfort bonus has paused)
report.title: 📋 Day {day} Report
report.noForecast: Tomorrow’s weather is unknown. (Place a radio to hear forecasts.)
report.winterPrep: ❄️ {n} days to winter — recommended fuel {fuel} · preserved food {canned}
brief.title: Today
brief.coldNow: 🥶 A cold snap rages. Wind bites through the cracks.
brief.coldDefended: 🔥 The cold snap holds, but your heating is holding too.
brief.coldSoon: 📻 A cold snap hits in {n} days.
brief.winter: ❄️ It is winter. Calories and fuel drain fast.
brief.advice.cold: Insulate and fire up the stove/heater against the cold.
brief.advice.winterPrep: Stockpile fuel and preserved food for the winter.
brief.advice.calm: All quiet outside today. Time to tend the shelter.
coldsnap.forecast: 📻 A cold snap is coming — in {n} days. Ready your insulation and heating.
coldsnap.hit: ❄️ A cold snap has hit. The temperature plunged.
coldsnap.exposed: 🥶 The cold wind bites through the cracks. Hunger sets in fast.
coldsnap.defended: 🔥 You weathered the cold snap by the stove.
front.forecast: 📻 The radio keeps repeating the same forecast. The great cold is {n} days out.
front.hit: 🌨️ The great cold has arrived. The deepest freeze of the winter.
front.exposed: 🥶 This cold doesn't care about walls. Hunger sets in fast.
front.defended: 🔥 The stove is holding the great cold at bay. It's still warm inside.
front.ended: 🌤️ The great cold has passed. The deepest freeze of the winter is behind you.
front.choice.title: The Great Cold
front.choice.body:
The radio was right. The next few days are the crux of this winter.
Decide how to get through them. Once decided, it holds until the cold breaks.
front.disc.ration.desc: Eat less, last longer. The stores stretch, but the house feels lean. (Hunger drains slower · Comfort -3)
front.disc.sleepless.desc: Tend the fire through the night to stretch the fuel. Sleep comes thin. (Heating fuel every other day · Sleep recovery -15)
front.disc.emergency.desc: Dig out the canned food you swore you wouldn't touch. It helps, for now. (Canned +{n} · Comfort -4)
front.disc.none.desc: No measures, nothing special — same as any other day. If you've prepared, that's enough.
front.disc.chose.ration: 🥫 Rations cut in half — until the cold breaks.
front.disc.chose.sleepless: 🕯️ Catnaps from tonight on. The fire stays lit.
front.disc.chose.emergency: 🥫 The emergency stash is open. You can see the bottom of the cupboard.
front.disc.chose.none: ❄️ No special measures. What you've prepared will have to do.
winter.passed: ❄️ You made it through winter number {n}.
demo.end.title: The First Winter Has Come
demo.end.body:
Day {d}. The first winter has begun. Will I make it through?
This is where the demo ends.
demo.end.sub: Nine winters await in the full game — twelve shelters, the metro line, and what lies beyond the blockade. Wishlist it and we'll let you know when winter comes.
demo.end.locked: The demo ends after the first winter — the story continues in the full game.
winter.page.title: Winter №{n}
winter.page.body:
Held out {days} days in the shelter.
{cold} cold snaps, {defended} of them held off.
Brought something back {exp} times.
Burned {fuel} fuel.
{cat}

{closing}
winter.page.body.noexp:
Held out {days} days in the shelter.
{cold} cold snaps, {defended} of them held off.
It was a winter you never set out to explore.
Burned {fuel} fuel.
{cat}

{closing}
winter.memoir.1: The first winter was just about lasting.
winter.memoir.2: A second winter. This time you were ready for it.
winter.memoir.3: The third. Your body knows the cold before it comes now.
winter.memoir.4: A fourth winter. Less enduring now, closer to living.
winter.memoir.5: The fifth. The sound of falling snow no longer frightens you.
winter.memoir.6: A sixth winter. This place has become familiar.
winter.memoir.7: The seventh. Winter is just a season that passes now.
winter.memoir.8: An eighth winter. This isn't a place you endure — it's home.
winter.memoir.9: The ninth winter. The one the title promised.
winter.memoir.beyond: Another winter. You are already past the nine you were promised.
winter.memoir.hurt.once: Got hurt once — {name}. One more scar.
winter.memoir.hurt.many: Got hurt {n} times. Your body will remember this winter.
winter.memoir.unhurt: Made it through this winter unhurt.
winter.memoir.front.ration: You got through the coldest days on half rations.
winter.memoir.front.sleepless: You kept the fire alive through the coldest days on catnaps.
winter.memoir.front.emergency: The coldest days took even the canned food you'd been saving.
winter.ninth.title: Nine Winters — And You Are Still Here
winter.ninth.body:
Nine winters, all weathered.

You survived to Day {day}.
Succeeded on {exp} expeditions, crafted {craft} things.
{cat}

The title of this game is now behind you.
ev.doctor.title: A Crackling Transmission
ev.doctor.text:
Deep in the night, the radio crackles on its own. Between the static, a human voice cuts in and out.

"…do you read me. …we saw the signal that held nine years. We are still watching. — Dr. ___"
ev.doctor.c0: You listen for a long while
ev.doctor.r0: The voice sinks back into static. But someone had been watching you.
report.tip.bandage: You have no bandages — find them in the residential district.
report.tip.water: You have no clean water — needed for cleaning and treatment.
report.tip.battery: No batteries for shelter power — explore the commercial district.
report.tip.clean: Cleanliness is low — 🧹 clean to restore comfort.
help.title: 🎓 Tutorial
help.exp.b: Pick a region on the map → gear up to raise success. 5/day; time passes when you return.
help.survive.b: Click a gauge = eat/drink/sleep. Out of ⚡ means 🛌 sleep. A bed gives deeper rest.
help.craft.b: Furniture is mainly crafted, not scavenged. Gather materials (cloth, parts, building material) on expeditions.
help.comfort.b: Furniture, light, cleanliness and a settled home build comfort, raising expedition success and recovery speed.
help.shelter.b: Each district has its own traits, upkeep and limits. Modify (rain catch, garden...) for self-sufficiency.
help.time.b: Weather shifts over days; seasons cycle every 12 days. Stockpile for winter. Saving is automatic.
help.camera:
Camera: drag or Q/E to rotate · right-click drag to pan · wheel to zoom (zoom out to see the ruins around you)
Progress saves automatically.
ev.wanderer.title: Wandering Survivor
ev.wanderer.text:
A survivor in rags stands before your shelter, hollow-eyed.
"...could you... spare a little food?"
ev.wanderer.r0:
The survivor bows and marks something on your map.
"I won’t forget this. That district still has supplies left."
(next expedition success +10%p)
ev.wanderer.r1: The survivor vanished into the dark without a word.
ev.trader.title: Traveling Merchant
ev.trader.text:
A merchant with a battered cart comes calling.
"Two batteries for a medicine set. What do you say?"
ev.trader.r0: "A fair trade." The merchant hands over the medicine and rolls his cart away.
ev.trader.r1: The merchant shrugs and moves on to the next shelter.
ev.dog.title: Stray Dog
ev.dog.text:
A scrawny dog approaches, tail wagging.
Somehow it survived in these ruins.
ev.dog.r0:
The dog wolfs it down and settles beside you.
It seems it will come along on your next expedition. (next expedition +10%p)
ev.dog.r1: The dog lingers a while, then limps away.
ev.storm.title: Storm Warning
ev.storm.text:
The sky looks ominous. Birds fly low, the air is heavy.
A gale is coming tonight.
ev.storm.r0:
You sealed the gaps and windows tight.
The wind raged all night, but the shelter held firm.
ev.storm.r1: Wind poured through the cracks all night, coating everything in grit. (cleanliness -10)
ev.broken.title: Gear Breakdown
ev.broken.text:
The flashlight switch is loose and a tool handle has cracked.
In the ruins, your gear is your life.
ev.broken.r0: Repaired good as new. Gear well kept is gear that keeps you alive.
ev.broken.r1: It’ll work, somehow — but you can’t quite trust it. (next expedition -5%p)
ev.thief.title: Signs of Intrusion
ev.thief.text:
Someone rifled around your shelter in the night.
Footprints are scattered everywhere.
ev.thief.r.safe:
Thanks to the lights being on, nothing was taken.
The glow must have scared the thief off. (the value of light!)
ev.thief.r.none: Luckily there was nothing worth taking... which is somehow sadder.
ev.seeds.title: A Strange Old Man
ev.seeds.text:
"Just two bottles of water. In return, take these seeds.
Seeds that still sprout, even in this world."
ev.seeds.r.green: You planted the seeds in the greenhouse garden. Astonishingly, they grew at once! (food +3)
ev.seeds.r.plain:
You took the seeds but have nowhere good to plant them. (food +1)
A greenhouse would have helped.
ev.seeds.r1: The old man clicks his tongue and hobbles off with his cane.
ev.radio.title: A Mysterious Broadcast
ev.radio.text:
A static-laced voice crackles from the radio.
"...coordinates... warehouse... supplies still remain..."
ev.radio.r0: You copied the coordinates onto your map. (double resources on next successful expedition)
ev.radio.r1: The broadcast, nothing but static, soon cut out.
ev.cat.title: A Gaunt Cat
ev.cat.text:
A gaunt cat slips in through the door and stares up at you.
Patchy fur, cautious steps — yet its eyes are clear and bright.
ev.cat.r0:
The cat licks the bowl clean and claims the coziest spot as if it were owed.
Now two hearts beat in this home. (comfort +6)
ev.cat.r1: The cat vanished into the dark. It seems you will never see it again.
ev.cat.coat.tabby: Once the dust was gone, a cheese-striped tabby coat showed through.
ev.cat.coat.black: Its coat was all black, and two amber eyes glinted within it.
ev.cat.coat.siamese: A cream body with chocolate ears and paws, and blue eyes—a Siamese.
ev.cat.coat.ragdoll: A white-cream coat with brown points and clear blue eyes—a Ragdoll.
ev.ending.title: A Visitor from the Sky
ev.ending.text:
A roar of rotors tore through the silence of the ruins. A helicopter.
A white-haired scientist in a hazmat suit steps down and walks toward you.
"I can’t believe it. Our satellite recorded your light for over 10,000 days.
The cleansed zone is finished — come with us."
ev.ending.r0: You nod slowly and take one last look back at your home.
ev.ending.r1:
"...it’s people like you who keep this planet alive."
The doctor leaves a radio behind and departs — call, and they’ll return anytime.
end3.title: A Knock at the Door
end3.text:
The ninth winter is behind you. On a night that smelled of spring, someone knocked.
Three figures in cold-weather gear. The insignia on their shoulders is one you've never seen. They were in no hurry.
"Come out when you're ready. Wherever you want to go."
end3.lean.escape: You find yourself thinking of every signal you sent into the sky. Somewhere, someone was listening to all of it.
end3.lean.newworld: You find yourself thinking of the papers you gathered. Observation, selection, assembly — you already know who these people are.
end3.lean.rest: You find yourself thinking of the room at your back. The stove, the blankets, the small things that crossed nine winters with you.
end3.r.escape: You stepped out the door and chose not to look back.
end3.r.newworld: You held out the invitation's coordinates. They nodded without a word.
end3.r.rest: You shook your head. They didn't ask twice.
end3.r.wait: They said they'd come again next spring. The door closed.
end3.early.title: An Unscheduled Transmission
end3.early.text:
The radio comes alive outside the regular hour. It's the doctor.
"A supply route passes near your coordinates next week. If you leave now, they can take you. ...You don't have to see all of it to the end."
end3.early.r0: You wrote down the coordinates. You don't have much to pack.
end3.early.r1: "Understood. That, too, is an answer." Through the static, the doctor seemed to smile.
end3.escape.line0: When the rescue craft's door closed, every sound fell away.
end3.escape.line1: Day {day}. That many mornings you met in that city.
end3.escape.line2: Through the window the city grows small. You can no longer see your own chimney smoke.
end3.escape.cat: On your lap, the cat folded its ears at the first engine sound it ever heard.
end3.escape.line3: The doctor said: because a light held out down there, the watching was never in vain.
end3.escape.line4: {winters} winters. {succ} expeditions. You made it out alive.{cat}
end3.newworld.line0: At the end of the coordinates stood a lit city. The first night with electricity you've seen since the burning.
end3.newworld.line1: Day {day}. They called your number before your name — and only then asked what your name was.
end3.newworld.line2: A warm room, an assigned place. The door locks from the inside — and from the outside.
end3.newworld.cat: The cat took a long time smelling the new room, as if deciding whether to approve.
end3.newworld.line3: Humanity starts again here, they say. By whose design, they don't say.
end3.newworld.line4: If this place is the price of {winters} winters endured — whether it's a reward or not, living here will tell.{cat}
end3.rest.line0: After they left, you closed the door and sat down by the stove.
end3.rest.line1: Day {day}. You started at the end of the world, and came this far.
end3.rest.line2: The old kettle is boiling. You couldn't leave this sound behind.
end3.rest.cat: The cat climbed onto your lap and settled in. Yes — we're staying.
end3.rest.line3: Rescue is not salvation. You are already alive — nine winters are the proof.
end3.rest.line4: {winters} winters. {succ} expeditions. And tomorrow morning, you will wake in this house again.{cat}
end3.note.escape: Boarded the rescue craft. The record of those winters stays here.
end3.note.newworld: Accepted the invitation. Off to become the next humanity, whatever that is.
end3.note.rest: Chose to stay. This house is the answer.
end3.after.escape: Ending — Escape. Written in the journal. This winter can still go on.
end3.after.newworld: Ending — New World. Written in the journal. This winter can still go on.
end3.after.rest: Ending — Rest. Written in the journal. Winter goes on.
ev.coldstranger.title: Warmth Beyond the Door
ev.coldstranger.text:
A cold-snap night. Someone lies curled and fallen beyond the door, still breathing.
They don’t answer when I shake them. They only shiver.
ev.coldstranger.c0: 🔥 Bring them in, share the fire (2 fuel)
ev.coldstranger.r0:
I set them by the stove until the frost left their skin. By morning the spot was empty.
A note wedged in the doorframe. One line of thanks. No name.
ev.coldstranger.note0: Took in the one outside and shared the fire.
ev.coldstranger.r1:
I closed the door. The wind swallowed whatever sound was left.
When light came and I opened it, only a pressed shape in the snow remained.
ev.coldstranger.note1: Pretended I never saw the one outside.
ev.spoilmerchant.title: The Half-Price Trader
ev.spoilmerchant.text:
Someone rolls up a cart. Canned goods, half price, they say.
The smile is a touch too eager. A few of the cans bulge at the seams.
ev.spoilmerchant.c0: Point at the swollen cans, wave them off
ev.spoilmerchant.r0:
I press a swollen seam. The trader turns the cart without a word.
Not fooled. That’s enough.
ev.spoilmerchant.note0: Spotted the spoiled cans and sent them off.
ev.spoilmerchant.r1ok: Two cans in hand. A shake says they’re fine. Lucky. (canned +2)
ev.spoilmerchant.r1bad: Two cans in hand. By nightfall my gut turned. One of them was rotten. (infection risk)
ev.leakyroof.title: Something Drips from the Ceiling
ev.leakyroof.text:
As the rain thickens, water begins to fall from one corner of the ceiling.
The pool on the floor spreads, wider and wider.
ev.leakyroof.c1: Just set a bucket under it
ev.leakyroof.r0: I braced the ceiling with scrap and cut off the trickle. The rain became an outside sound again.
ev.leakyroof.r1: Water seeped all night and the room turned damp. The walls carry a mildew smell now. (cleanliness -12)
ev.snowprints.title: Prints in the Night Snow
ev.snowprints.text:
I open the door to find prints circling the shelter through the snow.
No sign anyone looked in. They just went around once and trailed off yonder.
ev.snowprints.r0good: The tracks led to a bundle half-buried in snow. Its owner was long gone. (canned +1, cloth +1)
ev.snowprints.note0: Followed the tracks to an abandoned bundle.
ev.snowprints.r0bad: Following the tracks, I slipped on ice. My ankle throbs. I never did see who left them.
ev.snowprints.r1: I swept the prints clean. Whoever passed, it never happened now.
ev.lighthouseship.title: A Signal Far Out at Sea
ev.lighthouseship.text:
Deep in the night, a light blinks twice, far out at sea. A pause, then twice more.
Someone out there is watching the lighthouse.
ev.lighthouseship.c0: 🔥 Light the lamp and answer (1 fuel)
ev.lighthouseship.r0:
I flared the lamp and blinked twice. The far light answered twice, then slid slowly past the horizon.
No name, no meeting. Still, the night sea felt a little less wide.
ev.lighthouseship.note0: Answered the far sea-light with the lamp.
ev.lighthouseship.r1: The light blinked a while longer, then dimmed. Before long the sea went black again.
ev.greenhousebirds.title: Seed Thieves
ev.greenhousebirds.text:
A few birds have slipped through the cracked glass to peck at the garden seeds.
They don’t flee when they see me. They have the look of long hunger.
ev.greenhousebirds.r0: The birds burst up and vanished past the glass. The garden’s safe.
ev.greenhousebirds.r1: I scattered a handful and the birds gathered close. Fed, they lingered a long while before leaving.
ev.greenhousebirds.r1shiny: As I scattered the seed, one bird dropped something bright and flew off. A small part. (parts +1)
ev.greenhousebirds.note1: A bird dropped a shiny part behind.
ev.distantlight.title: A Distant Light
ev.distantlight.text:
A clear night. Somewhere beyond the ruins, a single light is lit.
It doesn’t blink, doesn’t draw nearer. It just is there.
ev.distantlight.r0:
I watched a long time. No way to know who lit it, or why.
Still — while that light burns, I am not the only one left in these ruins.
ev.distantlight.r0.rooftop:
Leaning on the rooftop rail, I watched a long while. Past the fallen skyline, one window is lit.
I nearly waved. It would never reach, of course.
ev.distantlight.r0.cabin:
Through the trees, a light flickers down in the valley. A person, or an animal’s eyes?
I didn’t go down. Some lights last longer kept at a distance.
ev.distantlight.r0.bunker:
Through a crack in the shell I looked to the far field. Toward the ruined city, one light burns.
Days of walking away, at least. Still — someone is out there.
ev.radioghost.title: A Voice Between Frequencies
ev.radioghost.text:
Late at night, turning the dial, a human voice catches between the static.
Speech or song, I can’t tell — it breaks and returns.
ev.radioghost.r0:
Cutting through the static, one broadcast came clear. — {title}
I set it down in the journal’s radio log.
ev.radioghost.r0none: I held the frequency, but it was all things I’d heard before. The voice sank back into static.
ev.radioghost.r1: I killed the dial and the room went quiet again. What it said, I’ll never know.
ev.oldcalendar.title: A Calendar on the Wall
ev.oldcalendar.text:
I found an old calendar page in a gap in the wall. A date from before the fall is circled.
What day it was, no one wrote down.
ev.oldcalendar.r0:
The back of the calendar is dense with notes. — {title}
I copied it into the journal.
ev.oldcalendar.r0none: The back held the same notes I’d already copied. I tucked it back into the wall.
ev.catgift.title: The Cat’s Gift
ev.catgift.text: The cat trots up with something in its mouth and drops it at my feet. A very proud face.
ev.catgift.r0rare: Two decent parts, from who knows where. I scratched its head. (parts +2)
ev.catgift.r0junk: A ragged scrap of cloth. Still, cloth is cloth. (cloth +1)
ev.catgift.r0rat: A dead rat. The cat looks immensely proud. I don’t know whether to laugh or cry.
ev.frozenpipe.title: A Frozen Pipe
ev.frozenpipe.text:
The temperature dropped hard overnight and the pipe froze solid. Tapping it, I hear ice catch inside.
Left alone, it might crack.
ev.frozenpipe.r0: I replaced the joint and wrapped it in insulation. The water runs again.
ev.frozenpipe.r1: I left it. Until the pipe thaws, the purifier will sit dead for a few days. (purifier stalled 3 days)
ev.frozenpipe.note1: Left the pipe frozen — the purifier stalls for days.
ev.caravanpass.title: A Procession Passing
ev.caravanpass.text:
Far along the ridge, a line of people passes. Carts and packs, a grown-up holding a child’s hand.
Never noticing me, they simply move on.
ev.caravanpass.r0:
The line never once stopped before it vanished over the ridge.
I didn’t call out, and they didn’t look back. It felt right that way.
ev.caravanpass.r0detail:
I watched a long while through the scope. Weary faces — yet the one in front keeps glancing back for the others.
Where they go, I can’t say. That they go together was the one clear thing.
ev.doctor.textFrag: …if you heard those logs, you understand now. One light that lasts nine years is enough.
memo.tagRegion.residential: From the residential zone — a daily life undone
memo.tagRegion.commercial: From the commercial zone — hoarding and riots
memo.tagRegion.industrial: From the industrial zone — the shutdown order
memo.tagRegion.slum: From the slums — those left behind
memo.tagRegion.bunker: From the bunker stairs — marks leading down
memo.tagRegion.subway: From the underground platform — those who went below
memo.tagRegion.resort: From the highland resort — the last vacationers
radio.heardNote: 📻 Heard a broadcast on the radio — {title}
bunker.roofHole: Rain leaks through the cracked ceiling. You can see the stars — but rainy days cost you.
bunker.roofTemp: A makeshift cover is up. No more leaks. Not quite whole yet.
bunker.roofFull: The ceiling is fully repaired. For the first time the bunker feels wholly snug. (comfort +4)
bunker.roofDoneStage1: Covered the ceiling for now. No more leaking rain.
bunker.roofDoneStage2: Fully repaired the ceiling. The bunker is whole now.
bunker.backdoorFound: Got hold of a cutter. Breach the back wall and the space beyond opens up.
bunker.backdoorDone: Cut the back wall open. A concrete anteroom — and stairs descending from its floor. (comfort +4)
bunker.stairToast: Nothing down there but darkness. …For now.
bunker.roofNote: Rain drove in through the ceiling hole.
rooftop.slateGapped: Two slates are gone; you can see sky. Rainy days cost you.
rooftop.slateFull: The slates are filled in; the roof is whole.
rooftop.slateDone: Filled in the missing slates. No more leaking rain.
rooftop.slateNote: Rainwater seeped through the gaps in the slates.
rooftop.gardenHarvest: 🌱 Harvested food from the rooftop garden (+{n})
rooftop.gardenDormant: 🌱 Winter — the rooftop garden lies dormant.
cutter.foundNote: 🔩 Found a cutter in the industrial zone.
gun.foundNote: 🔫 Found a working gun in a collapsed guard post. {dur} rounds.
gun.emptyNote: 🔫 The magazine is empty. Needs servicing at the workbench.
hostile.soundNote: 🌫️ Heard someone moving deeper in the ruins. Came back without crossing paths.
hostile.traceNote: 🔥 Passed a cold campfire. Someone was here first.
hostile.robbedNote: 🕶️ Men blocking the road took part of the haul. You backed away.
hostile.metNote: 🕶️ Ran into strangers on the road. Seeing you empty-handed, they let you pass.
hostile.gunNote: 🔫 You raised the gun and they backed off. {dur} rounds left.
hostile.hurtNote: 🩸 You broke free, but it cost you.
proj.intro: Work that takes days — a little material at a time. Each day I get only as far as my hands reach.
proj.memoir.title: The Year’s Work
proj.clearPassage.name: Clearing the Blocked Passage
proj.clearPassage.stage1: Big stones block the foot of the stairs. I roll them aside, one by one.
proj.clearPassage.stage2: I sweep out the last rubble and grit. The passage begins to take shape.
proj.clearPassage.workNote: Cleared the rubble at the foot of the undercroft stairs, stone by stone.
proj.clearPassage.doneToast: The rubble is all cleared. A cold draft seeps from beyond the passage. …I can’t go in yet.
proj.clearPassage.memoir: Day {day}. Over several days I cleared the rubble that blocked the foot of the undercroft stairs. Beyond is still dark, but now the air moves through. Will there come a day I go down there?
proj.breakwaterHut.name: Breakwater Hut
proj.breakwaterHut.stage1: I clear the debris washed up at the breakwater’s end. First, level the ground.
proj.breakwaterHut.stage2: I raise a frame from salvaged timber — braced against the sea wind.
proj.breakwaterHut.stage3: I wall it in and lay the roof, sealing it with tarp.
proj.breakwaterHut.workNote: Made some progress on the hut at the breakwater’s end.
proj.breakwaterHut.doneToast: The breakwater hut is done. Dock work goes faster now, and I can cut one more ice-fishing hole.
proj.breakwaterHut.memoir: Day {day}. I built a small hut at the end of the breakwater. Days of leveling rubble, raising a frame, laying a roof. This harbor feels a little more like mine now.
map.regionUnlockHarbor: The harbor district appears on the map.
icefish.title: Ice Fishing
icefish.action: Cut a hole in the ice and drop a line
icefish.caught: Pulled {n} fish from the ice hole.
icefish.salt: Scraped {n} salt crystal from the hole’s rim.
icefish.bottle: An old bottle came up on the line. A damp note inside.
icefish.noSpot: No more holes to cut today.
ev.smuggler.title: The Smuggler
ev.smuggler.text: A strange boat touches the pier a moment. A hooded man unpacks his cargo, glancing at me. Not one to linger.
ev.smuggler.r0: I take a can of fuel. He counts his price and heads back to the boat.
ev.smuggler.r0winter: Winter rates, he says — triple the price. Fuel has never cost me this much.
ev.smuggler.r1: I hand over the salt; he tosses me two parts. Harbor salt is worth something.
ev.smuggler.r2: He shrugs and weighs anchor. The boat vanishes into the fog.
subway.hubDesc: Repair the hand-car and restore the route map — unlocks rail recovery and the black market
subway.hubDone: This platform is a hub now
subway.hubDoneToast: I fixed the platform into a hub. Hung the dusty route map back on the wall. Bring the rails back to life, and that dark becomes a road.
subway.blizzardBlocked: Snow has buried the surface routes. Only opened subway lines get through.
subway.mushroomHarvest: 🍄 Picked {n} mushroom from the bed in the dark.
subway.mushroomDry: No water — the bed dried out. Nothing to pick today.
subway.marketIntro: A faceless counter. Only notes and goods change hands — surplus for something rarer.
proj.subRail1.name: Rail Recovery · Segment 1 (Residential)
proj.subRail1.stage1: I clear the caved-in rubble blocking the rails. The first shovel is the heaviest.
proj.subRail1.stage2: I pull the rotted ties and lay new ones, gauging the spacing by fingertip in the dark.
proj.subRail1.stage3: I couple the rails and bolt them down. I roll the hand-car to the end — it runs through.
proj.subRail1.workNote: Brought the Residential-bound rails a little further back to life.
proj.subRail1.doneToast: Segment 1 is open. A shortcut to Residential runs through the dark now — half the time, and no blizzard closes this road.
proj.subRail1.memoir: Day {day}. Opened the first segment toward Residential. Cleared rubble, laid ties, coupled rails. The hand-car rolls through the dark. However deep the snow, this one road stays open.
proj.subRail2.name: Rail Recovery · Segment 2 (Commercial)
proj.subRail2.stage1: A deeper segment. I start by bailing out the flooded rubble.
proj.subRail2.stage2: I lay the ties. Water running down the walls is my only company.
proj.subRail2.stage3: I bolt the rails and check the joints. The way to Commercial is through.
proj.subRail2.workNote: Made progress on the Commercial-bound rails.
proj.subRail2.doneToast: Segment 2 is open. Commercial is within reach underground now.
proj.subRail2.memoir: Day {day}. Opened the Commercial-bound segment. Days spent bailing standing water. This dark is slowly becoming my road.
proj.subRail3.name: Rail Recovery · Segment 3 (Industrial)
proj.subRail3.stage1: The farthest segment. Even fallen ceiling has to be cleared.
proj.subRail3.stage2: I set ties along a long straight run. I can’t see the end of it.
proj.subRail3.stage3: I join the last rail. I push the hand-car, and the cold air of the Industrial yard rolls in.
proj.subRail3.doneToast: Segment 3 is open. The line reaches the far Industrial yard — winter can’t wall me in now.
proj.subRail3.memoir: Day {day}. Opened the last segment, toward the Industrial yard. Cleared collapsed ceiling, laid ties down a long straight. Three lines now reach under the dark. The city’s veins still beat — I brought them back.
proj.cablecar.name: Cable Car Recovery
proj.cablecar.stage1: Clear the collapsed platform debris and raise the pylons. The mountain wind is fierce.
proj.cablecar.stage2: String the snapped cable back across the cliff. My hands go numb.
proj.cablecar.stage3: Hang the gondola and mend the drive. With a creak, one car moves.
proj.cablecar.workNote: Worked on the cable car recovery.
proj.cablecar.doneToast: The cable car runs again. The climb to the highland is far shorter now.
proj.cablecar.memoir: Day {day}. I brought the cable car back. Days of clearing rubble, stringing cable, hanging a gondola. The mountain feels a little closer now.
proj.observatory.name: Observatory Build
proj.observatory.stage1: Pour a concrete base on the hill. At night the sky feels unusually close.
proj.observatory.stage2: Raise the dome frame in a ring. Starlight leaks between the ribs.
proj.observatory.stage3: Cap the dome and seat the telescope. I open the slit, and the sky comes in.
proj.observatory.doneToast: The observatory is done. On clear nights the sky will show me things now.
proj.observatory.memoir: Day {day}. I built an observatory on the hill. Poured a base, raised a dome, seated a telescope. On clear nights now, I stand a long while and watch the sky.
highland.altitudeFuel: 🏔️ The altitude burned {n} more fuel to stay warm.
highland.altitudeCold: 🏔️ Ran out of altitude heating fuel — the hearth’s warmth fades.
avalanche.forecast: 🏔️ A low rumble rolls off the ridge. An avalanche is near — think twice before climbing today.
avalanche.blocked: 🏔️ An avalanche buried the path. The highland resort is closed for a few days.
avalanche.cleared: 🏔️ The snow has settled. The way to the highland is open again.
avalanche.blockedToast: The path is blocked by avalanche. It reopens in {n} days.
avalanche.title: 🏔️ Avalanche Warning
avalanche.body: The ridge is rumbling. The usual route to the resort is unsafe. You can take the risky detour up, or turn back for today.
avalanche.detour: ⚠️ Take the risky detour (success -15%p · slower · loot ×1.5 · injury risk)
avalanche.turnback: Turn back for today (highland closed for a few days)
avalanche.turnedback: You turned back. The highland is closed until the avalanche passes.
avalanche.detourLoot: ⚠️ The risk paid off — you reached what others couldn’t. The haul is generous.
avalanche.detourHurt: ⚠️ You slipped on the detour’s icy path.
nightsky.foundNote: 🌌 On a clear night, the sky showed something — you sketched “{title}” in your notebook.
memo.tagRegion.research: From the forbidden zone — records past the cordon
memo.tagRegion.harbor: From the grounded liner — how the deck shanty came to be
forbidden.intro: This is beyond the cordon. Without a suit, not one step in.
hazmat.name: Hazmat Suit
hazmat.craftHint: Needed to enter the forbidden zone (durability {dur} · wears each expedition)
hazmat.crafted: You made the hazmat suit and put it on. Durability {dur}.
hazmat.craftedNote: 🥽 Finished the hazmat suit — you can cross the cordon now.
hazmat.firstHint: Past the cordon — start at the checkpoint.
hazmat.repaired: You mended the suit. Back to {dur} durability.
hazmat.needCraft: You need to make a suit first.
hazmat.blocked: You can’t cross the cordon without a hazmat suit.
hazmat.wornOut: The suit is worn out. Mend it before going back in.
fallout.cleared: The fallout has settled. The cordon can be crossed bare-handed now, and the City Core beyond it has opened on the map.
mastery.up: The paths of {name} feel familiar now — geography {stars}. You’ll see more from here on.
fallout.barehandHurt: Walked inside the cordon with no suit — the residual radiation left its mark.
hazmat.wearNote: 🥽 The suit wore down a little (durability {dur} left).
gun.name: Old Gun
gun.durLine: {dur}/{max} rounds — keeps the city center's nights at bay
gun.needFind: No gun to service. There should be one somewhere in the city center.
radio.broadcastHint: Send out what you gathered, one at a time (energy {e} · sent {sent}/{total} · lights {lit})
radio.needBase: You need to raise the radio base first.
radio.allSent: You’ve sent out all you had.
radio.sentLit: 📡 You sent the signal out. Far off on the map, one more light comes on — {n} now.
radio.sentNoLit: 📡 You sent the signal out. It spreads into the dark.
proj.radioBase.name: Radio Base Recovery
proj.radioBase.stage1: I raise the fallen antenna mast again, turning the dish back to the sky.
proj.radioBase.stage2: I wire the transmitter back together, reviving the broken circuits one by one.
proj.radioBase.stage3: I hook up the power and battery lines. For the first time, a needle twitches on the gauge.
proj.radioBase.workNote: Made some progress recovering the radio base.
proj.radioBase.doneToast: The radio base has come alive. Now I can send what I gathered up there.
proj.radioBase.memoir: Day {day}. I brought the radio base beyond the cordon back to life. Days of raising the antenna, wiring the transmitter, hooking up power. Even from this ruin, I can send out one signal now.
proj.clearPassage.revealNote: 🕯️ The dark beyond the cleared passage was an underground way leading below the cordon. I can go down there now.
ev.doctorReg.title: A Regular Contact
ev.doctorReg.text:
The radio base has linked to the station in orbit. Through the static, the voice comes in clear now, at a set hour.

"Signal received. I’m counting the lights you lit, from up here. That a place has lasted nine winters — that gives us a reason to come down."
ev.doctorReg.textTruth: "…so you read all the files. Then you know who I was. I’m the one who couldn’t stop that winter — and so, the one who watches your light to the end."
ev.doctorReg.c0: You trade word for a long while
ev.doctorReg.r0: The contact sinks into static. But this signal no longer sounds alone.
ev.ending.textSignal: The radio operator adds: "We followed your signal. We saw them too — the lights coming on across the map, one by one."
truth.title: The Truth of That Day
truth.p1:
First it was a sickness. They locked the cities, and drove people inward. Whether the cordon was to save people or to hold them in — by the end, even they no longer knew.
The models kept missing. The spread was always a little faster.
truth.p2:
There was only one way to stop it, they wrote. Erase the innermost ring with fire. Knowing the soot it left would call down years of winter, they pressed it anyway.
The names of the three who dissented were struck out. One of them stayed down here.
truth.p3:
That one could not go down, so they chose to watch from above — counting ground signals from the orbital station. As long as one light held out, they wrote, this was not a failure.
Nine winters, and the air would settle, was the calculation. And they waited, nameless, for the one light that would still be burning by then.
jnl.help.p1.title: A Worn Notebook
jnl.help.p1.body:
Someone lived in this container before you. You found a worn notebook under the bed.
The first page is scrawled in a hurry.

🎒 Expeditions — pick a region on the map and gear up to raise your odds. Up to 5 a day; time passes when you return.
jnl.help.p2.title: The Second Page
jnl.help.p2.body:
The handwriting settles a little — notes on how to get by.

🥫💧⚡ Survival — click a gauge to eat, drink, or sleep. Run out of energy and you must 🛌 sleep. A bed makes for far deeper rest.
jnl.help.p3.title: A Stained Page
jnl.help.p3.body:
This page is stained — flecks of building material still cling to it.

🔨 Crafting — furniture is mostly built, not found. Gather cloth, parts and material, then craft it at the workbench.
😊 Comfort — furniture, light, cleanliness and a settled home build comfort, boosting expedition success and recovery.
jnl.help.p4.title: A Folded Corner
jnl.help.p4.body:
A dog-eared corner — this page must have been read often.

🏠 Shelters — every district has its own traits, upkeep and limits. Mods like rain catches and garden boxes help you become self-sufficient.
🌤️ Seasons — weather shifts over a few days, and seasons turn every 12 days. Stock up before winter arrives.
jnl.help.p5.title: The Last Page
jnl.help.p5.body:
Only one line on the last page — written in haste, but carefully all the same.

Camera: drag or Q/E to rotate · right-click drag to pan · wheel to zoom (zoom out to see the ruins around you)

Progress saves automatically. And this notebook — it's yours now.
jnl.tut1.title: Day 1 — The First Page
jnl.tut1.body:
A hurried note on the notebook's first page.

"If you don't know where to start — deal with thirst first. It wears you down faster than hunger ever will."

💧 Click a gauge to drink. Furniture in your inventory can be clicked to place it on the floor — click a placed piece again to rotate or reclaim it.
jnl.tut2.title: Day 2 — A Second Note
jnl.tut2.body:
The next page has a map sketched by hand.

"The pantry doesn't fill itself. Open the map and start with the safer regions. Gearing up first makes it far safer."

🎒 Use the backpack icon on the HUD to open the expedition map.
jnl.tut3.title: Day 3 — A Third Note
jnl.tut3.body:
A short line added at the bottom of the page.

"Scavenging alone won't carry you far — craft what you need at the workbench. And clean now and then; no one lasted long living in filth."

🔨 Workbench and 🧹 Clean buttons are on the HUD.
tip.rain: "Set something up to catch the rain — what the sky gives is free." — a rain catch collects water on rainy days.
tip.snow: "Snow makes a shelter colder than it looks. Consider getting some insulation." — comfort drops in bad weather, so plan ahead.
tip.injury: "Don't just tough out an injury. Left alone, it festers." — treat injuries from the expedition panel once you have the materials.
tip.event: "Now and then a stranger knocks. The same answer isn't always right." — weigh each encounter's choice on its own.
tip.energy: "Don't push on until your eyes blur. That's your body telling you to sleep." — rest with sleep once energy runs low.
tip.winter: "Winter's here. For those who didn't stock up, it's a long, bitter season." — hunger drains faster and gardens freeze in winter.
tip.stack: "Furniture with a flat top can hold smaller things on it." — try placing small items on top of tables and the like.
tip.freshfood: Fresh food doesn't keep. No fridge? Eating it today is how you save it.
quest.head: To Do
quest.drink.text: Click the 💧 gauge to drink water
quest.eat.text: Click the 🥫 gauge to eat something
quest.place.text: Turn on 🔧 edit mode and place 1 furnishing (bed recommended)
quest.depart.text: Open the 🎒 map and set out on your first expedition
quest.craft.text: Craft anything once at the 🔨 workbench
quest.doneToast: 🎉 Now it's up to you to survive
quest.drink.lore: First page — 'Water first, always. Thirst kills your judgment before it kills you.'
quest.drink.done: Your body wakes a little. The notebook's owner was right.
quest.eat.lore: 'An empty stomach won't last half a day. And eat the fresh stuff first — without a fridge, it won't see tomorrow.'
quest.eat.done: The can tastes awful. It also tastes like being alive.
quest.place.lore: 'To call it home, put something in it. I started with a bed. Settle where you sleep, and the heart follows.'
quest.place.done: The room looks a little less empty. Strangely reassuring.
quest.depart.lore: 'Go out while the sun is high. Start with the houses nearby. Greed comes back as injuries.'
quest.depart.done: Out the door. Half of every day lives outside.
quest.sleep.lore: 'When the strength is gone, sleep. No way around it — the body keeps the ledger, and sleep settles it.'
quest.sleep.done: Your eyelids are heavy. Morning will sort the day out for you.
quest.craft.lore: 'What you scavenge is only material. Hands make it into things. Two cloth makes one bandage.'
quest.craft.done: The feeling of making something. It's been a while.
quest.clean.lore: 'Last — keep it clean. Sickness moves into a dirty home before you do.'
quest.clean.done: The notebook's first chapter ends here. The rhythm of these days is yours now.
